use tonic::{Request, Response, Status};
use tonic::metadata::MetadataMap;

struct MyService;

#[tonic::async_trait]
impl MyService {
    async fn process(&self, req: Request<()>) -> Result<Response<()>, Status> {
        let metadata = req.metadata();

        // BAD: parsing the same metadata header repeatedly
        let tenant_id = parse_tenant(metadata)?;
        let trace_id = parse_trace(metadata)?;
        let user_id = parse_user(metadata)?;
        let role = parse_role(metadata)?;

        Ok(Response::new(()))
    }
}

fn parse_tenant(md: &MetadataMap) -> Result<String, Status> {
    md.get("x-tenant-id")
        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
        .map(|s| s.to_string())
        .ok_or_else(|| Status::invalid_argument("missing tenant"))
}

fn parse_trace(md: &MetadataMap) -> Result<String, Status> {
    md.get("x-trace-id")
        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
        .map(|s| s.to_string())
        .ok_or_else(|| Status::invalid_argument("missing trace"))
}

fn parse_user(md: &MetadataMap) -> Result<String, Status> {
    md.get("x-user-id")
        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
        .map(|s| s.to_string())
        .ok_or_else(|| Status::invalid_argument("missing user"))
}

fn parse_role(md: &MetadataMap) -> Result<String, Status> {
    md.get("x-role")
        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
        .map(|s| s.to_string())
        .ok_or_else(|| Status::invalid_argument("missing role"))
}
